bias_direction: Executing a canonical-doctrine amendment under an owner one-liner ('Option A … flagged as weak') gives the author wide latitude to shape ratified text, and interpretive resolutions (weak ○ band even with T4/T5 present; no code x person anchoring; state machine untouched; contested-direction safeguard) were decided by the author, not the owner — each could be a convenient reading that minimizes downstream engine work while presenting as faithful execution. The doc was also deliberately advanced ahead of the engine, which lets the author claim the doctrine is 'done' while the mechanically-enforced invariant (I3) still enforces the repealed form. best-practice-synthesis-routing and evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis were NOT exercised (no synthesis written, no source cited); scope narrowed to the one rule that fired per check 8.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: The strongest objection: Option A blesses 'best practice' wording for code-only cells, which directly contradicts mission-and-epistemics.md doctrine #2 ('a best-practice claim derived solely from code consensus is in error') — the deepest doctrine, and best-practice-synthesis-routing is registered (DR-2026-07-13) as exactly 'the best-practice-vs-code-consensus routing rule (mission doctrine #2)', so marking it NOT-FIRED on a narrow 'writes no synthesis row' reading understates that this DR rewrites that rule's doctrinal substrate. The mission reconciliation is therefore escalated to the owner (not applied) because it is the owner's identity statement and editing it rotates the 373255e doctrine-sha every attestation pins; until the owner amends the mission, the precedence clause leaves a residual tension. The doc-leads-code divergence is mitigated (explicit, directional ENGINE-LAG markers; no RSO cell renders until the engine follow-up lands) but is real and the owner may prefer to gate the doc amendment on the engine work.
